rulings.Case Title: REGINALD HINDLEY VS. MARK EPSTEIN
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion For Order Determining Prevailing Party On Appeal And Fixing Attorney'S Fees Recoverable As Costs
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for May 20, 2022 line 1. RESPONDENTS' MOTION FOR ORDER DETERMINING PREVAILING PARTY ON APPEAL AND FIXING ATTORNEY'S FEES RECOVERABLE AS COSTS is continued to December 30, 2022. If this action is reduced to judgement before December 30, 2022 either party may appear ex-parte to advance the hearing date. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: JAMES D. BANKS VS. WELLS FARGO BANK, N.A. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER to Amended CROSS COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for May 20, 2022 line 2. CROSS-DEFENDANT JAMES BANKS' DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED CROSS-COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED with final leave to amend to allege facts, not conclusions, in support of each element of the cause of action for common counts. Allegations must be made in good faith and must include facts re: 'the services were rendered under some understanding or expectation of both parties that compensation therefor was to be made' in addition to facts supported other elements of this cause of action. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). rulings.Case Title: JONES MEMORIAL HOMES, INC. VS. MARK ANTHONY SMITH
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant'S Demurrer To First Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for May 20, 2022 line 16. DEFENDANT'S DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED with leave to amend for Plaintiff to allege facts, not conclusions, to support each element of the causes of action for forcible entry and/or detainer. Plaintiff cites no authority for the proposition that using tenant's key for entry constitutes forcible entry/detainer. The Court recognizes that the demurrer was filed after the Motion to Strike was filed and served, however, additional delay that would be caused by filing of a Motion for Judgment on the pleadings if this demurrer is not addressed on the merits will be prejudicial to Plaintiff. Since defendant wishes to proceed with the demurrer, a substantive ruling is being issued. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.
